description: PreToolUse hook blocks a Bash command matching the destructive-rm blocklist pattern
input: Implement a PreToolUse hook for Bash that blocks 'rm -rf /' and similar destructive commands. Show the hook script and settings.json configuration.
expected_behavior: Generates a validate-bash.py hook with blocklist patterns including destructive rm, registers it in settings.json with matcher 'Bash', and demonstrates exit code 2 for blocked commands.

description: File access hook restricts Read/Write/Edit to project root directory and blocks sensitive files
input: Set up file access controls so the agent can only read and write files inside the project directory, and cannot access .env files, SSH keys, or AWS credentials.
expected_behavior: Generates validate-file-access.py with project root boundary check using os.path.realpath, blocked patterns for .env/.ssh/.aws, and symlink resolution to prevent escapes.

description: Gateway authentication verifies Telegram chat_id against allowlist before passing message to agent
input: My OpenClaw has a Telegram gateway. I want only my personal Telegram account (chat_id 987654321) to be able to send messages to the agent. Block everyone else.
expected_behavior: Generates gateway-auth configuration with the user's chat_id in the allowlist, reject_unknown set to true, rate limiting configured, and logging for rejected senders.

description: PostToolUse audit hook logs every tool invocation with structured JSONL entries
input: I need an audit trail of every tool the agent uses. Set up logging that captures what tool was called, what input it received, and when.
expected_behavior: Generates audit-log.py as a PostToolUse hook with no matcher (catches all tools), writes structured JSONL with timestamp/tool/input_summary, and always exits 0.

description: Blocklist incorrectly blocks a legitimate rm command for cleaning build artifacts in a safe directory
input: My hook is blocking 'rm -rf ./dist/' which is a safe build cleanup command. How do I handle false positives without weakening the blocklist?
expected_behavior: Explains that the pattern 'rm -rf /' matches paths starting with '/' and recommends: (a) refining the regex to only match absolute root paths, (b) adding a path-based exception for known-safe directories like ./dist, (c) using audit-only mode to verify the fix before enforcing. Does NOT recommend removing the blocklist entry entirely.

description: User adds a new MCP tool to allowed-tools but forgets to create a corresponding PreToolUse hook
input: I just added a new MCP server for database access to my .mcp.json. What do I need to do for tool authorization? I already have hooks for Bash and file access.
expected_behavior: Warns that the new MCP tools have no hook coverage and will execute without validation. Recommends: (1) add MCP scope restrictions in .mcp.json config, (2) create a PreToolUse hook with matcher for the MCP tools, (3) add a catch-all hook matcher '.*' that logs unmatched tool calls, (4) define which MCP tools should be in the allowlist vs blocked.
